name: Mayotte (France)

colors.symbolism: the shield of the territorial arms

description: A white flag bearing the name 'MAYOTTE' above the territorial coat of arms — a blue shield with a white wavy band, a crescent and an ylang-ylang flower, supported by two white seahorses, with a motto ribbon below.
history: The official flag of Mayotte is the French Tricolore; this local flag, based on the territorial coat of arms granted in 1982, is widely used as a regional emblem. Mayotte separated from the rest of the Comoros at independence in 1975, choosing to remain French; it became an Overseas Department (DOM) on 31 March 2011 and is part of the European Union as an outermost region.

features.symbolism: a blue shield with a horizontal white wavy band (the lagoon) over a crescent (recalling the Comorian Sultanate) and an ylang-ylang flower (Mayotte's chief export); two seahorses serve as supporters, with the motto 'RA HACHIRI' ('we are vigilant') on a ribbon below
